Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Wayde van Niekerk out of Paris Olympics 400m – report

Wayde van Niekerk’s name has disappeared from the World Athletics list of qualified 400m athletes for the 2024 Paris Olympics.

There was confusion late on Wednesday evening whenWayde van Niekerk’sname disappeared from the World Athletics list of qualified400mathletes for the2024 Paris Olympics.

Van Niekerk is the current world record holder in the event having claimed the gold medal in the one-lap race at the 2016 Rio de Janeiro Olympics when he stopped the clock in a time of43.03.

Due to the fact the 31-year-old will no longer compete in the 400m individual race, he will beunavailablefor the 4x400m relay, a race in which South Africa stood a legitimate chance at winning a medal.

With Van Niekerk missing,Lythe PillayandZakithi Nenewill be Team South Africa’s entrants in the men’s 400m.

According toBusinessLIVE, a SA Sports Confederation and Olympic Committee (Sascoc) official has confirmed the news.

Van Niekerk will reportedly compete in the 200m instead and be a reserve for the 4x100m relay.

It’s unlikely he will have any shot at a medal in the 200m, let alone make the final.

Van Niekerk’s career best over the 200m distance is 19.84 which he set back in 2017.

No fewer than 17 different athletes have run under 20-seconds this year alone – and seven of them have posted sub-19.90 times.

Currently Van Niekerk isn’t even among the three South Africans who have qualified with automatic entry times for the Paris Olympics.

Shaun Maswanganyi, Luxolo Adams and Benjamin Richardson are those to have qualified.

Richardson beat Van Niekerk in this year’s 200m at the SA championships.

Curiously, Van Niekerk’s agentPeet van Zylsaid he knew nothing about his client’s reported withdrawal from the 400m, while at least one national team coach was also said to be in the dark regarding the news.

The Paris Olympics 2024 will start on27 July.

The heats of the men’s 400m will take place on 4 August with the final scheduled for 7 August.

The heats of the men’s 200m will take place on 5 August with the final scheduled for 8 August.
